zh.lifeSummary: 當Julien Margaret Hurst於1500年出生在英國戴文艾可謝特時，她的父親Sir William Martin Hawarde54歲，母親Christiana Paulet52歲。她與Sir Richard William Martin結婚。他們至少是15個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1570年4月6日在英國多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Julien Margaret Hurst于1500年出生在英国戴文艾可謝特时，她的父亲Sir William Martin Hawarde54岁，母亲Christiana Paulet52岁。她与Sir Richard William Martin结婚。他们至少是15个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1570年4月6日在英国多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hurst
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived near a wood or wooded hill, from Middle English hirst(e), herst(e), hurst(e) (Old English hyrst) or a habitational name from any of the places so called, such as Hurst Green (in Mitton, Lancashire), Hirst (Northumberland), Hurst (Berkshire, Kent, Warwickshire), Hurstpierpoint (Sussex), or Hirst in Longwood (Yorkshire).
Irish: re-Anglicized form of de Horsaigh, the Gaelicized form of the English habitational name Horsey , established in Ireland since the 13th century.
German and Swiss German (also Hürst): topographic name from Middle High German hurst ‘woodland, thicket’; or a habitational name from a place so named in Westphalia.

variants.name: Herst
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or nickname from Polish herszt ‘ringleader, chieftain’.

variants.name: Horsey
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from Horsey Pignes (Somerset), Horse Eye (Sussex), or Horsey (Norfolk), from Old English hors ‘horse’ + ēg ‘island, low-lying land’.

variants.name: Hearst
meanings: Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of some similar (like-sounding) original Ashkenazic surname such as   Hirsch  .

variants.name: Horst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Phares, Alois, Armin, Fritz, Hans, Inge, Manfred, Monika, Reinhold.

North German and Dutch: topographic name from Middle Low German hurst, Middle Dutch horst ‘undergrowth, brushwood, wild place’. More specifically, the term was also used to denote a crow's or similar large bird's nest, a raised area in surrounding marshland, or an area of uncleared woodland, all of which meanings could have contributed to the surname. This surname is also a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. Compare Hurst .

History: As a name of German origin (see 1 above) the surname Horst is established among the American Mennonites. Their progenitors were the brothers Joseph, Michael, and Peter Horst, who came to PA in 1731 together with their widowed mother. Among their descendants the altered form Hurst is also found.

variants.name: Hirst
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hurst  .

nameMeanings.name: Julien
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Julian  , now also found in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Julien Margaret Hurst was born in 1500, in Exeter, Devon, England, her father, Sir William Martin Hawarde, was 54 and her mother, Christiana Paulet, was 52. She married Sir Richard William Martin. They were the parents of at least 15 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 6 April 1570, in Athelhampton, Dorset, England, at the age of 70.

ko.lifeSummary: Julien Margaret Hurst(이)가 1500년 영국 데폰 세터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Martin Hawarde(은)는 54세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Christiana Paulet(은)는 52세였다. 그녀는 Sir Richard William Martin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1570년 4월 6일 영국 도르세트 앳헬함프턴에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.
